name: Haivision Video Streaming API
what_it_does: Haivision Video Infrastructure REST API for live video encoding, streaming, and enterprise broadcast automation. Enables AI agents to manage video encoder configuration and live stream initiation for broadcast automation, handle SRT (Secure Reliable Transport) stream management for low-latency live video delivery, access video transcoding and adaptive bitrate management for OTT streaming, retrieve stream health monitoring and quality metrics for broadcast operations, manage cloud video processing and encoder orchestration for scalable live events, handle content ingest from satellite, SDI, and IP sources for broadcast contribution, access CDN integration and video delivery management for large-scale streaming, retrieve latency and quality analytics for broadcast engineering, manage multicast and unicast delivery configuration for enterprise video distribution, and integrate Haivision with broadcast playout, media asset management, and CDN platforms.
use_cases: Automating live stream configuration from broadcast agents
Managing SRT stream delivery from broadcast operations agents
Monitoring stream health from video quality agents
Scaling live events from cloud encoding agents
not_for: Consumer video streaming without broadcast engineering context
Recorded video-only platforms without live streaming needs
Small organizations without enterprise broadcast infrastructure
best_when: A broadcast network, enterprise video team, or live event producer using Haivision wants AI agents to automate encoder configuration, stream management, quality monitoring, and multi-site video delivery.
avoid_when: BROADCAST RELIABILITY RISK: Haivision manages live broadcast streams — automated encoder configuration changes during active broadcasts can interrupt live streams causing audience impact; apply changes during pre-show or maintenance windows. DMCA/copyright — automated live stream monitoring must detect and respond to unauthorized content (copyrighted music, sports blackout violations); automated live stream of copyrighted content without license creates DMCA liability. Export control (ITAR/EAR) — Haivision encryption technology (SRT protocol) may be subject to US export control regulations; automated cross-border video streaming must consider ITAR/EAR encryption export requirements.

auth.notes: Haivision uses API key and basic authentication. REST API with JSON. Documentation at doc.haivision.com. No native webhooks. Montreal, Quebec HQ (Canada). Founded 2004 by Mirko Wicha. Public (HAI TSX). Haivision is the creator of SRT (Secure Reliable Transport) open source protocol. Enterprise and broadcast video market. Strong in military, government, and broadcast. Competes with Elemental (AWS), Harmonic, and Imagine Communications for broadcast encoding.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual enterprise license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual per-encoder or per-channel license; cloud services subscription
pricing.notes: Montreal, QC, Canada. Founded 2004. Public (HAI TSX). SRT protocol creator. Enterprise and broadcast video. Military/government and broadcast focus. Competes with Elemental/AWS and Harmonic.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; hardware vs cloud differ

known_agent_gotchas: LIVE BROADCAST DISRUPTION RISK: Automated encoder configuration changes during active live streams interrupt broadcast; automated changes must detect stream state and only apply during pre-show or maintenance windows

DMCA copyrighted content — automated live stream operations must implement content identification for copyrighted music and sports blackout compliance; streaming unauthorized copyrighted content creates DMCA takedown and liability risk

ITAR/EAR encryption export — Haivision SRT and HEVC encryption may be subject to US Export Administration Regulations; automated cross-border streaming with encryption may require export license for restricted country destinations
No webhooks — stream health and event notification requires polling; no native push for encoder status, stream start/stop, or quality degradation events
Hardware vs cloud API — Haivision has both hardware encoders (Makito series) and cloud services with different APIs and management interfaces; integration planning must specify deployment model

Military/government clearance — Haivision serves DoD and classified government video; some encoder features and configurations are restricted; verify feature availability for non-classified deployments
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise broadcast video. SOC2, FIPS. API key/basic auth. US/EU/CA. Live stream and broadcast data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Broadcast video. SOC2, FIPS. API key. US/EU/CA. Live stream and broadcast infrastructure.
